text_eng:
On the morning when soap bubbles rained down, she began to feel it.
She heard herself ask: ‘Where does this anguish come from?’

She suspected it stemmed from the paradox between the urge to return to her routine and the desire to spend more time with those who filled her life with meaning.

All the encounters, and even the missed encounters, had already left her feeling a sense of longing.
She longed for what she had not yet experienced and felt nostalgia for the moments yet to come.

She wished the space-time continuum would dissolve so that her days might continue to be filled with those small exchanges of affection.

Perhaps these were merely the reflections of a sentimental rationalist trying to understand the science of the soul.

It took her a while to realise that, filled with tenderness, she was feeling again.
And that perhaps the meaning of life was simply that. To feel.

Translated with DeepL.com (free version)
text_original:
Na manhã em que choveu bolhas de sabão, começou a senti-la.
Ouviu-se perguntar: "De onde vem essa angústia?"

Suspeitava vir do paradoxo entre a vontade de voltar a rotina e o desejo de ficar por mais tempo com aqueles que enchiam sua vida de significado.

Todos os encontros, e mesmo os não encontros, já haviam deixado saudades.
Sentia saudades do que não tinha vivido e nostalgia dos momentos que ainda estava por viver.

Gostaria que a interface espaço-tempo se dissipasse para que seus dias continuassem sendo preenchidos pelas pequenas trocas de afeto.

Quem sabe essas fossem apenas reflexões de uma racionalista sentimental tentando compreender a ciência da alma.

Demorou a perceber que, preenchida de ternura, voltava a sentir.
E que talvez o sentido da vida seja simplesmente esse. Sentir

text_eng: Back when the Barbenheimer trend was at full strength and Oppenheimer won the Oscars, I wanted to also watch the movie so I can hate on it knowing that I couldn't be exposed for not having seen it. And boy did I hate it. Having an over 3 hours long movie is already bold, but saying practically the same thing all over again from start to finish is completely outragious. Cilian Murphy was fine, but other than that it was the most pretentious intelectual masturbation I had ever seen. Everything was so cringe and just screaming to the audience: I'm so deep! I also hate the grandomanious Chrispoher Nolan, who really does everything to make his movies big and impressive. What happened to the good old minimalism? Saying a lot with little means? God, and what is even that deep about it? Crazy how many people liked that shitty move.

My topic for this course touches on randomness, and coincidentally (though most probably not coincidence at all), I am also considering randomness as part of another project for another course. I am a great admirer of serendipity. Actually, depending on your world view, you could consider every event in life to be random, and then there are happenings that you consider destiny. Every person you cross in the supermarket is random and negligible, but as soon as it is that certain person and it is not at all their area, it seems like a very random but symbolic experience. Destiny is just the selection of random events you choose to give value to, right? I still remember the quote by Milan Kundera, even though I read that book many years ago: Chance and chance alone has a message for us. Everything that occurs out of necessity, everything expected, repeated day in and day out, is mute. Only chance can speak to us.
Maybe it is just a very romantic view on life to give certain events a deeper meaning, to attach yourself, your day, your memory, your attention to this unforeseeable moment. Sometimes you have to make room in your everyday life to allow these encounters to happen, to let go of control and embrace everything that comes your way with the intention of it being something important. Sometimes it occurs in a moment where you least expect it, like in the supermarket from the example above. Some people do not think about the chain of causal events that may have led to the random situation, while others try to manipulate their and other people’s life to converge into a desired event. Maybe seeing something as random enhances its value? Appreciating the minute probability of it happening as if it meant it could still be taken away from you, as if the low chances also meant little time and presence. Or does it reduce the value of a person or a moment, as stochasticity equals meaninglessness? 
Anyways, just random thoughts on this topic ...

ringing alarm
gives a rhythm 
that surprises
groginess and fatigue 
comes unrested 
mind starts 
screaming 
with urgency
awareness rises 
as air thickens 
lethargy overwhelms 
the consciousness
sunlight enters 
sending 
the first 
signal of crisis

light shines in 
its gentle warmth 
wakes me up 
the smell of 
meadow 
mixed with coffee 
reaches the bed 
before i do 
rested i am 
the body
heavily filled with 
peace and joy 
i step up 
setting a new 
rhythm 
to this beautiful 
morning

The feeling in the chest 
i prescribe on this text
stuck on the back of my throat
the insurmountable load
so, where does it begin
and where does it end? 
does it begin when the ache carries?
does it begin when the time hurries?
does it begin when the boundary is crossed?
does it begin when the mind is lost? 
does it begin when the body is abused?
does it begin when the pride is bruised?
does it begin when the situation permits? 
does it begin when the trouble commits?
does it begin when the loneliness comes? 
does it begin when the silence numbs?
does it begin when the world is arranged? 
does it begin when the sacrifice is exchanged?
does it begin when the shame takes form?
does it begin when the heart is torn?
does it begin when the space is displaced? 
does it begin when the pain surfaced?
does it begin when the bitter blood boils?
does it begin when the quiet joy soils?
does it begin when there is no end?

This is called Inexpressible.

A feeling in the chest, 
pressed into this text. 
Stuck on the back of my throat
an insurmountable load.

So where does it begin? 
And where does it end?
Perhaps when the ache carries, 
or when time hurries. 
A boundary is crossed; 
the mind is lost. 
The situation permits it, 
and the trouble commits it.
Then loneliness that comes, 
with a silence that numbs. 
The world so deranged, 
a sacrificial exchange. 
Shame takes its form, 
as the pride readies to storm.
A safe space is displaced; 
a sanctuary erased. 
The body is abused, 
the pride is bruised. 

Bitter blood boils, 
and quiet joy spoils.
It begins… when there is no end.

The stages have been set 
What was necessary has been said
No tears, no laughter, just a nod
An approval that it’s time to take a stop
The conversation marked the beginning of relief
But I have to be honest it comes with grief
What is there to do now? 
Is there any redemption to restoring a vow?
A vow of friendship
A bow of a ship
Will I see you in a better light again?
Will our friendship ever rekindle again?
